dsl_rule.translation_notes: Morning star pattern requires 3-day analysis. Entry conditions: Day 1 closes lower than open (bearish), Day 2 (star) closes higher than Day 1 close, Day 3 closes higher than Day 2 close. The optional doji requirement on Day 2 cannot be precisely expressed with allowed indicators—doji detection would require body_pct near 0, but this is approximated in the pattern structure itself. Exit uses a simple reversal condition (close below previous close). Maximum hold set to 3 days to align with the 3-day pattern duration.

Entry conditions: Day 1 closes lower than open (bearish), Day 2 (star) closes higher than Day 1 close, Day 3 closes higher than Day 2 close. The optional doji requirement on Day 2 cannot be precisely expressed with allowed indicators—doji detection would require body_pct near 0, but this is approximated in the pattern structure itself. Exit uses a simple reversal condition (close below previous close). Maximum hold set to 3 days to align with the 3-day pattern duration.

source_rule_text: A morning star bullish reversal pattern occurs when the first day closes lower than open, the second day (star) closes higher, and the final reversal day closes even higher; significance increases if the star is a doji.
